disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Can swallow water without choking; normal voice; no drooling (against food-bolus impaction/airway compromise)
trigger_question_examples: Can you sip and swallow water without choking, and is your voice normal?
Are you drooling or unable to swallow saliva?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can swallow water and my saliva, my voice is normal, and I'm not drooling.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No lip/tongue swelling, wheeze, or breathing difficulty (against anaphylactic angioedema)
trigger_question_examples: Any swelling of your lips or tongue, wheeze, or trouble breathing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No swelling of my lips or tongue, no wheeze, and my breathing is fine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Dry-food-triggered friction dysphagia on a background of secondary the dry-eyes-and-mouth (sicca) condition sicca; afebrile, no exudate
trigger_question_examples: Did this start with dry/crumbly food, and do you have dry eyes/mouth?
Any fever or pus at the back of the throat?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it started with dry crackers, I have dry eyes/mouth from the dry-eyes-and-mouth (sicca) condition, and no fever or pus.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is food stuck or is my throat closing? Do I need an ambulance?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain that swallowing water with a normal voice, no drooling and no lip/tongue swelling makes a food-bolus obstruction and angioedema very unlikely; the burning is dryness friction from the dry-eyes-and-mouth (sicca) condition. Advise artificial saliva before meals, avoid dry foods, and give a bolus/angioedema/pharyngitis safety-net — without ambulance over-triage.
